what: What is a monetary policy shock?
title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: Fed, Bank England, ECB announces rate decision, part move expected markets part . unexpected part economists call monetary policy shock: exogenous change policy lets empirical macroeconomists separate causal effect policy policy’s response economy. Every paper running impulse response function (IRF), local projection (LP), vector autoregression (VAR), event study needs shock series identifying input. Shadow-rate measures play role zero-lower-bound episodes, policy rate pinned floor. series live today XLS CSV files scattered across individual authors’ personal pages, Federal Reserve research data pages, GitHub mirrors, inconsistent column names, date formats, update cadences. three decades methodology measuring shocks evolved: narrative reading FOMC minutes (Romer Romer 2004), high-frequency identification around announcement windows (Kuttner 2001; Gurkaynak, Sack, Swanson 2005; Nakamura Steinsson 2018), sign-restricted decompositions joint rate--stock moves (Jarocinski Karadi 2020), orthogonalisation pre-announcement public data Fed’s Greenbook forecasts (Bauer Swanson 2023; Miranda-Agrippino Ricco 2021). Shadow-rate term-structure models (Wu Xia 2016) address zero-lower-bound regime separately. technique produces subtly different series, modern empirical papers increasingly report impulse responses several robustness check. mpshock centralises series tidy data frames provenance, loaded one call, network required.

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: mpshock empirical macroeconomists, central-bank research staff, rates strategists, PhD students. Typical uses supplying identifying input local projection, VAR, proxy-SVAR, event study asset-price responses FOMC, MPC, RBA announcements. specification needs exogenous monetary policy shock, package supplies .

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
code: install.packages("mpshock")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/mpshock")

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: Every call mpshock follows pattern: ask series name, work tidy data frame. ’s guided tour main functions. Output shown #> prefix see console.

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
code: # Attach the package library(mpshock)  # mp_list() returns a metadata table with one row per bundled series # (we select a subset of columns here for a readable display) mp_list()[, c("series", "author", "type", "start", "end", "n")] #>                    series                                           author        type      start        end   n #> 1      nakamura_steinsson                    Nakamura and Steinsson (2018)       shock 2000-02-01 2014-03-01 170 #> 2           bauer_swanson                         Bauer and Swanson (2023)       shock 1988-02-01 2023-12-01 431 #> 3              gss_target Gurkaynak, Sack, Swanson (2005) / Swanson (2021)       shock 1991-07-01 2015-10-01 292 #> 4                gss_path Gurkaynak, Sack, Swanson (2005) / Swanson (2021)       shock 1991-07-01 2015-10-01 292 #> 5    jarocinski_karadi_mp                     Jarocinski and Karadi (2020)       shock 1990-02-01 2024-01-01 404 #> 6   jarocinski_karadi_cbi                     Jarocinski and Karadi (2020) information 1990-02-01 2024-01-01 404 #> 7 miranda_agrippino_ricco               Miranda-Agrippino and Ricco (2021)       shock 1991-01-01 2019-06-01 342 #> 8                  wu_xia                                Wu and Xia (2016) shadow_rate 1960-01-01 2022-02-01 385

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: ns mp_shock object (inherits data.frame). date column set first day month shock column percentage points. Months FOMC meeting coded zero, matching convention Gertler-Karadi (2015) authors’ maintained releases.
code: # Load the Nakamura-Steinsson policy news shock into the object `ns` ns <- mp_shock("nakamura_steinsson")  # head() shows the first six rows plus a provenance header head(ns) #> <mp_shock> nakamura_steinsson [monthly, 6 obs, 2000-02-01 to 2000-07-01] #>        date      shock #>  2000-02-01  0.0215744 #>  2000-03-01 -0.0028683 #>  2000-04-01  0.0000000 #>  2000-05-01  0.0335943 #>  2000-06-01 -0.0061341 #>  2000-07-01  0.0000000

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: Always cite underlying paper use series academic work. See Citation full list.
code: # mp_source() prints the paper, DOI, source URL, and method description mp_source("nakamura_steinsson") #> nakamura_steinsson #>   Nakamura and Steinsson (2018) #>   DOI: 10.1093/qje/qjy004 #>   URL: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/HZOXKN #>   Policy news shock: first principal component of five interest-rate futures #>   surprises in a 30-minute window around FOMC announcements, aggregated to #>   monthly.

text: January March 2008 rows capture inter-meeting 75-basis-point cuts stand largest surprises GFC window.
code: # Subset to the 2008 financial-crisis months using start and end arguments crisis <- mp_shock("nakamura_steinsson",                    start = "2008-01-01",                    end   = "2008-06-30") head(crisis) #> <mp_shock> nakamura_steinsson [monthly, 6 obs, 2008-01-01 to 2008-06-01] #>        date      shock #>  2008-01-01 -0.0692382 #>  2008-02-01  0.0000000 #>  2008-03-01  0.2043770 #>  2008-04-01 -0.0755810 #>  2008-05-01  0.0000000 #>  2008-06-01 -0.0252113

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: method = "mean" (arithmetic mean within quarter) method = "end" (last month’s value, used stock-like series Wu-Xia shadow rate) also supported.
code: # Aggregate the monthly series to quarterly by summing within each quarter ns_q <- mp_to_quarterly(ns, method = "sum")  head(ns_q) #> <mp_shock> nakamura_steinsson [quarterly, 6 obs, 2000-01-01 to 2001-04-01] #>        date      shock #>  2000-01-01  0.0187061 #>  2000-04-01  0.0274602 #>  2000-07-01  0.0126884 #>  2000-10-01  0.0537947 #>  2001-01-01 -0.1058595 #>  2001-04-01  0.0328807

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: every bundled series single shock column. Wu-Xia shadow rate carries three data columns: first-differenced shock pipeline compatibility, level shadow_rate, effective federal funds rate effr reference.

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: common robustness check: run regression different identification strategies see whether impulse responses line . ’s line two shock series common monthly grid. correlation 0.4 0.7 range typical: series agree biggest FOMC surprises diverge harder--classify meetings. Papers report IRF results least two shock series robustness (Brennan, Jacobson, Matthes, Walker 2024, FEDS 2024-011).
code: # Load two alternative US monetary policy shocks over the same window ns <- mp_shock("nakamura_steinsson", start = "2005-01-01", end = "2013-12-31") bs <- mp_shock("bauer_swanson",      start = "2005-01-01", end = "2013-12-31")  # Merge by date; attach suffixes so the two shock columns can be told apart panel <- merge(   ns[, c("date", "shock")],       # NS shock series   bs[, c("date", "shock")],       # BS orthogonalised surprise   by = "date",   suffixes = c("_ns", "_bs") )  head(panel)  # Pearson correlation of the two shock series over the common sample cor(panel$shock_ns, panel$shock_bs, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: Wu-Xia series ends February 2022 Atlanta Fed paused update policy rates normalised. series remains standard reference two zero-lower-bound episodes (2008-12 2015-12 2020-03 2022-02). UKMPD live-maintained Bank England flagship UK source post-1997 work.

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: Users aware caveats. per-series help file (?nakamura_steinsson, ?bauer_swanson, etc.) discusses depth. Shock versus surprise. “Surprise” refers raw market reaction announcement (e.g. NS first principal component, raw Bauer-Swanson MPS); “shock” refers exogenous structural component removing predictable public information (e.g. MPS_ORTH, Miranda-Agrippino-Ricco residual, Jarocinski-Karadi MP). mpshock uses shock column name pipeline uniformity; consult series’ docs underlying object . Information-effect debate. Nakamura-Steinsson (2018) “Fed information effect” interpretation challenged Bauer Swanson (2023, AER) Miranda-Agrippino Ricco (2021). estimating macro IRFs, MPS_ORTH (bauer_swanson) informationally-robust shock (miranda_agrippino_ricco) current methodological leaders; NS JK-MP remain widely cited. Monthly aggregation. event-study series aggregated calendar-month summation; months FOMC meeting coded zero. Bu, Rogers, Wu (2021, JME) recommend coding -meeting months NA proxy-SVAR/LP-IV avoid downward-biased variance. mpshock apply ; apply post hoc needed. Wu-Xia sensitivity. Shadow-rate estimates depend lower-bound choice, number factors, yield maturities (Krippner 2020, JMCB). Robustness Krippner’s alternative SSR recommended result relies shadow-rate level.

title: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics
text: Euro-area shocks: Jarocinski-Karadi ECB version, Altavilla et al. (2019) Euro-Area Monetary Policy Database. Romer Romer (2004) US narrative shocks. Bolhuis, Das, Yao (2024 IMF WP) cross-country panel 29 central banks’ HFI shocks. vignette reproducing Gertler-Karadi (2015) style impulse response using mpshock plus lpirfs. Brennan, Jacobson, Matthes, Walker (2024) cross-series comparison helper.

what: Bauer-Swanson orthogonalised monetary policy surprise — bauer_swanson
title: Bauer-Swanson orthogonalised monetary policy surprise — bauer_swanson
text: monthly orthogonalised monetary policy surprise series (MPS_ORTH) Bauer Swanson (2023), raw MPS alongside. orthogonalised series removes predictability public economic information available FOMC meeting, isolating genuinely exogenous monetary-policy innovation.

title: Bauer-Swanson orthogonalised monetary policy surprise — bauer_swanson
Bauer-Swanson orthogonalised monetary policy surprise — bauer_swanson
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. Orthogonalised MPS, percentage points. mps_raw numeric. Raw high-frequency MPS summed within month, percentage points. series character. Series identifier "bauer_swanson".

title: Bauer-Swanson orthogonalised monetary policy surprise — bauer_swanson
text: Bauer, M. D., & Swanson, E. T. (2023). "Reassessment Monetary Policy Surprises High-Frequency Identification." NBER Macroeconomics Annual 37: 87-155. doi:10.1086/723574 . Data: https://www.frbsf.org/research--insights/data--indicators/monetary-policy-surprises/.

title: Bauer-Swanson orthogonalised monetary policy surprise — bauer_swanson
text: Orthogonalisation. MPS_ORTH OLS residual regressing raw MPS six pre-announcement predictors: surprise recent nonfarm payrolls release, trailing 12-month employment growth, log S&P 500 change prior three months, change 10-year minus 2-year Treasury slope window, log commodity-price- index change, Bauer-Chernov option-implied 10-year Treasury skewness. See Bauer Swanson (2023) Appendix Table .1 exact predictor definitions data sources. Relation nakamura_steinsson. series use first principal component tight-window futures surprises. MPS_ORTH additionally removes predictability public data, Bauer- Swanson argue isolates policy shock "Fed response news" NS attribute Fed information effect. Critique. Hoesch, Rossi, Sekhposyan (2023) show alternative orthogonalisation choices yield different residuals. Fed informational advantage, orthogonalisation throws away construction. Vintage. Bundled FRBSF-maintained update covering December 2023. frozen vintage, download directly source URL mp_source().

what: Beckers Australian narrative monetary policy shock — beckers_au
title: Beckers Australian narrative monetary policy shock — beckers_au
text: Narrative Romer-Romer-style monetary policy shock Australia. RBA cash-rate changes purged systematic response Bank's internal forecasts (Bishop-Tulip 2017 methodology) augmented credit-spread information separate genuine policy innovations responses financial conditions. Quarterly frequency. headline bundled shock Beckers's preferred credit-spread-augmented series ("BT-CS"); pre-augmentation Bishop-Tulip series (bt) included alongside comparison.

Bishop-Tulip pre-augmentation shock, percentage points. rate_chg numeric. Raw quarterly cash-rate change, percentage points. series character. Series identifier "beckers_au".

title: Beckers Australian narrative monetary policy shock — beckers_au

BT-CS series regresses cash-rate changes RBA internal forecasts (GDP, unemployment, CPI) plus measures domestic credit spreads, quarterly frequency. residual identified policy shock. Frequency note. quarterly-frequency series currently bundled mpshock. mp_to_quarterly() unnecessary; pass beckers_au directly quarterly VARs LPs. Licence. RBA research output Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

title: Cesa-Bianchi-Thwaites-Vicondoa UK high-frequency shock — cesa_bianchi_uk
text: Identification. Surprises extracted change three-month sterling interbank rate 60-minute window around MPC announcement, isolating unexpected component policy decision. Event-level values aggregated monthly summation; months MPC meeting coded zero. Superseded. identification strategy richer asset-price menu ongoing maintenance, use ukmpd. CTV series remains useful historical reference comparisons pre-UKMPD empirical literature. Vintage. Static published version (1997-06 2015-01). extension maintained authors.

title: Cloyne-Hurtgen UK narrative monetary policy shock — cloyne_hurtgen_uk
text: Narrative identification. Cloyne Hurtgen (2016) read Bank England Inflation Report forecasts regress Bank Rate change Bank's real-time projections output, unemployment, inflation horizons two years. residual "narrative" shock. bundled series extension carried forward Cesa-Bianchi, Thwaites, Vicondoa using methodology; original paper covers 1975 2007 bundled vintage CTV re-compiled version 1997-06 onwards. Comparison HFI. Narrative shocks typically lower- frequency high-frequency event-window surprises capture broader policy reassessments. can differ materially cesa_bianchi_uk ukmpd even common sample.

title: GSS path factor (Swanson extended) — gss_path
text: See gss_target identification, rotation sensitivity, aggregation details; caveats apply factors since come joint rotation. Regime coverage. path factor picks forward-guidance surprises informative Swanson factor zero-lower-bound period (2009 2015). variance rises sharply window relative pre-ZLB sample, consistent forward guidance becoming dominant policy tool.

title: GSS target factor (Swanson extended) — gss_target
text: Federal Funds Rate factor Swanson's (2021) three-factor decomposition high-frequency FOMC surprises, direct extension original "target" factor Gurkaynak, Sack, Swanson (2005). Event-level factor values summed within calendar months; months scheduled FOMC meeting coded zero.

title: GSS target factor (Swanson extended) — gss_target

text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. GSS target factor, percentage points. series character. Series identifier "gss_target".

title: GSS target factor (Swanson extended) — gss_target
text: Swanson, E. T. (2021). "Measuring Effects Federal Reserve Forward Guidance Asset Purchases Financial Markets." Journal Monetary Economics 118: 32-53. doi:10.1016/j.jmoneco.2020.09.003 . Data: https://sites.socsci.uci.edu/~swanson2/. Original two-factor decomposition: Gurkaynak, R. S., Sack, B., Swanson, E. T. (2005), International Journal Central Banking 1(1): 55-93.

title: GSS target factor (Swanson extended) — gss_target
text: Identification. Swanson (2021) computes first three principal components high-frequency futures surprises, rotates () zero loading factor 3 current-month fed-funds-rate surprise, (ii) minimum sum squared factor-3 values pre-ZLB sample 1991-07 2008-12, (iii) sign normalisation. target path factors therefore conditional pre-ZLB window used pin factor 3. Extensions past bundled span must re-estimate rotation, simply append new events. Relation GSS 2005. Pre-2009 target factor closely tracks original two-factor decomposition Gurkaynak, Sack, Swanson (2005). Post-2009 differs unconventional policy announcements absorbed distinct LSAP factor (bundled v0.1.0; see Swanson's website full three-factor panel). Monthly aggregation. Event-level factors summed within calendar months. Months scheduled FOMC meeting coded 0. Users want distinguish "news" "news = 0" recode -meeting months NA estimation (Bu, Rogers, Wu 2021, Journal Monetary Economics 118).

text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. Action factor (current cash-rate surprise), percentage points. action numeric. Current-meeting cash-rate surprise, percentage points. path numeric. Forward-guidance surprise, percentage points. term_premium numeric. Long-end term-premium surprise, percentage points. series character. Series identifier "hambur_haque_au".

title: Hambur-Haque Australian monetary policy shock — hambur_haque_au
text: Hambur, J., & Haque, Q. (2023). "Monetary Policy Transmission, Real Interest Rates Credit Spreads: Evidence Australia." Economic Record (2024). doi:10.1111/1475-4932.12786 . Data: Reserve Bank Australia Research Discussion Paper 2023-04, https://www.rba.gov.au/publications/rdp/2023/2023-04/. CC 4.0.

title: Hambur-Haque Australian monetary policy shock — hambur_haque_au
text: Identification. Three PC components rotated isolate () action, current-meeting cash-rate surprise; (ii) path, forward-guidance surprise expected short rates; (iii) term_premium, residual long-end move attributable duration / term-premium effects. shock set action pipeline compatibility; users running multi-factor IRFs use individual component columns. Coverage. bundled series spans April 2001 December 2019, matching published paper. RBA released maintained extension covering COVID-era LSAP period. Licence. Published RBA Research Discussion Paper Commonwealth Australia Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International licence.

what: Jarocinski-Karadi central bank information shock — jarocinski_karadi_cbi
title: Jarocinski-Karadi central bank information shock — jarocinski_karadi_cbi
text: "central bank information" shock Jarocinski Karadi (2020), identified alongside pure MP shock sign restrictions joint rate-stock response. information shock moves short rates stock prices direction, interpreted central bank revealing private information economy. Monthly US series authors' maintained update.

title: Jarocinski-Karadi central bank information shock — jarocinski_karadi_cbi
Jarocinski-Karadi central bank information shock — jarocinski_karadi_cbi
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. CB information shock (CBI_median), percentage points. series character. Series identifier "jarocinski_karadi_cbi".

title: Jarocinski-Karadi central bank information shock — jarocinski_karadi_cbi
text: Jarocinski, M., & Karadi, P. (2020). "Deconstructing Monetary Policy Surprises: Role Information Shocks." American Economic Journal: Macroeconomics 12(2): 1-43. doi:10.1257/mac.20180090 . Updated data: https://github.com/marekjarocinski/jkshocks_update_fed_202401.

title: Jarocinski-Karadi central bank information shock — jarocinski_karadi_cbi
text: See jarocinski_karadi_mp full identification scheme caveats. information shock companion component: markets interpret hawkish rate surprise sign Fed seen positive economic news, stocks rise rather fall. Controversy. Bauer-Swanson (2023) Acosta (2023) argue information shock largely artefactual: omitted-variables problem (Fed markets reacting pre-meeting public data) plus weak sign-restriction identifier. Interpret caution; information-effect literature central result, read critiques citing.

what: Jarocinski-Karadi pure monetary policy shock — jarocinski_karadi_mp
title: Jarocinski-Karadi pure monetary policy shock — jarocinski_karadi_mp
text: "pure" monetary policy shock Jarocinski Karadi (2020), identified via sign restrictions joint response short-term interest rates stock prices around FOMC announcements. median decomposition allows MP information shocks co-occur. Monthly US series authors' maintained update.

title: Jarocinski-Karadi pure monetary policy shock — jarocinski_karadi_mp
Jarocinski-Karadi pure monetary policy shock — jarocinski_karadi_mp
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. Pure MP shock (MP_median), percentage points. series character. Series identifier "jarocinski_karadi_mp".

title: Jarocinski-Karadi pure monetary policy shock — jarocinski_karadi_mp
text: Jarocinski, M., & Karadi, P. (2020). "Deconstructing Monetary Policy Surprises: Role Information Shocks." American Economic Journal: Macroeconomics 12(2): 1-43. doi:10.1257/mac.20180090 . Updated data: https://github.com/marekjarocinski/jkshocks_update_fed_202401.

title: Jarocinski-Karadi pure monetary policy shock — jarocinski_karadi_mp
text: Identification. Two high-frequency surprises enter: 3-month fed-funds futures S&P 500, 30-minute windows around FOMC announcements. 2-shock SVAR identified sign restrictions: positive MP shock raises rates lowers stocks (negative co-movement); positive CB-information shock raises (positive co-movement). Median vs poor-man's decomposition. "poor-man's" variant sorts events sign pattern assigns surprise wholly one shock. median variant solves set-identified problem picks rotation whose impulse responses lie median admissible rotations; shocks can co-occur every event. mpshock uses median version (MP_median, CBI_median). Results set-identified, point-identified: users report robustness across rotations. Critical follow-. Acosta (2023, "Perceived Causes Monetary Policy Surprises") argues rate-stock sign pattern weak discriminator policy information shocks two typically negatively correlated high frequency regardless shock type. Bauer Swanson (2023) argue information shock reflects omitted pre-announcement data rather genuine Fed private information. Users estimating information-effect IRFs report robustness critiques.

title: Miranda-Agrippino-Ricco informationally-robust MP shock — miranda_agrippino_ricco
text: informationally-robust monetary policy shock Miranda- Agrippino Ricco (2021), constructed component FF4 (fourth Eurodollar futures) high-frequency surprises orthogonal Fed's Greenbook information set. Isolates exogenous policy moves shifts central bank's private information economy. Monthly US series Degasperi Ricco maintained extension.

title: Miranda-Agrippino-Ricco informationally-robust MP shock — miranda_agrippino_ricco
Miranda-Agrippino-Ricco informationally-robust MP shock — miranda_agrippino_ricco
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. Informationally-robust MP shock, percentage points. info numeric. Companion information component, percentage points. series character. Series identifier "miranda_agrippino_ricco".

title: Miranda-Agrippino-Ricco informationally-robust MP shock — miranda_agrippino_ricco
text: Miranda-Agrippino, S., & Ricco, G. (2021). "Transmission Monetary Policy Shocks." American Economic Journal: Macroeconomics 13(3): 74-107. doi:10.1257/mac.20180124 . Updated data: https://github.com/riccardo-degasperi/info-policy-surprises.

title: Miranda-Agrippino-Ricco informationally-robust MP shock — miranda_agrippino_ricco
text: Construction. raw FF4 surprise (3-month-ahead fed-funds futures) projected Fed's Greenbook / Tealbook forecast revisions GDP, unemployment, inflation horizons zero four quarters (Miranda-Agrippino Ricco 2021, Section III). residual informationally-robust monetary policy shock. Extension past 2013. published paper covers 1991 2009; Degasperi-Ricco maintained update extends June 2019. Fed's Tealbook subject five-year release embargo, post-2013 observations use real-time SPF Greenbook-equivalent series instead. strictly information basis published 1991 2009 series. Ramey critique. Ramey (2018, discussion Miranda-Agrippino-Ricco NBER Summer Institute) notes orthogonalisation respect Fed's information set, market's. news markets infer announcement beyond Tealbook remains residual. Weak-instrument F-statistics drop materially 2007.

TRUE, non-matching target rows get 0 shock column instead NA. Useful target complete monthly panel missing shocks treated zero-surprise months. Defaults FALSE.

                                                                                                                                                                 description #> 1                                      Policy news shock: first principal component of five interest-rate futures surprises in a 30-minute window around FOMC announcements, aggregated to monthly. #> 2                                                          Orthogonalised monetary policy surprise (MPS_ORTH): high-frequency FOMC-event surprise purged of predictability from public information. #> 3                                            Federal Funds Rate factor (extended): the Swanson (2021) three-factor decomposition analogue of the original GSS target factor, aggregated to monthly. #> 4                                                Forward Guidance factor (extended): the Swanson (2021) three-factor decomposition analogue of the original GSS path factor, aggregated to monthly. #> 5                                                 Pure monetary policy shock (median decomposition): high-frequency FOMC surprise identified by sign restrictions on the joint rate-stock response. #> 6                                                          Central bank information shock (median decomposition): the component of FOMC surprises co-moving rates and stocks in the same direction. #> 7                                                             Informationally-robust monetary policy shock: FF4 high-frequency surprise orthogonalised against the Fed's Greenbook information set. #> 8                                 Shadow federal funds rate: the Wu-Xia estimate of what the fed funds rate would have been during zero-lower-bound episodes. Shock column is the first difference. #> 9  UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (UKMPD): three-factor decomposition (Target, Path, QE) of high-frequency surprises around Bank of England MPC announcements. Live-maintained by the BoE. #> 10                                                 UK high-frequency monetary policy surprise: Kuttner-style tight-window surprise around Bank of England MPC announcements, aggregated to monthly. #> 11                            UK narrative monetary policy shock: Romer-Romer-style identification using Bank of England Inflation Report forecasts to purge the cash rate of systematic reactions. #> 12                                       Australian monetary policy surprise: three-component decomposition (action, path, term premium) of OIS and AGS yield moves around RBA cash-rate decisions. #> 13     Australian narrative monetary policy shock: cash-rate changes orthogonal to RBA internal forecasts, augmented with credit-spread information (Beckers's 'BT-CS' headline series), quarterly.

what: Load a monetary policy shock series — mp_shock
title: Load a monetary policy shock series — mp_shock
text: Loads named shock series bundled package tidy data frame class mp_shock. Optionally filters date range.

title: Load a monetary policy shock series — mp_shock
Load a monetary policy shock series — mp_shock
text: series Character(1). Name series. See mp_list() available series. start, end Optional Date character (YYYY-MM-DD) filters returned series. NULL, returns full bundled span.

title: Load a monetary policy shock series — mp_shock
text: data frame class c("mp_shock", "data.frame") columns: date: Date, first day observation month. shock: numeric, shock value units published source (see mp_source() per-series help files units scaling conventions). series: character, series identifier. series carry additional columns. bauer_swanson returns mps_raw alongside shock (orthogonalised surprise); miranda_agrippino_ricco returns info; wu_xia returns shadow_rate effr.

title: Load a monetary policy shock series — mp_shock
text: Aggregation. event-study series (nakamura_steinsson, bauer_swanson, gss_target, gss_path, jarocinski_karadi_mp, jarocinski_karadi_cbi, miranda_agrippino_ricco) bundled monthly frequency summing FOMC-event-level surprises within calendar month. Months scheduled FOMC meeting coded zero, matching convention Gertler Karadi (2015) authors' maintained releases. Bu, Rogers, Wu (2021, Journal Monetary Economics 118) argue -meeting months coded NA rather zero estimating proxy-SVAR LP-IV models, avoid downward-biased variance weak-instrument F-statistics. mpshock apply adjustment; recode loading needed. Scaling. Units differ across series. nakamura_steinsson rescaled one-year nominal Treasury-yield equivalents; bauer_swanson others raw percentage-point surprises. See series' help file.

title: Load a monetary policy shock series — mp_shock

Aggregate a monthly shock series to quarterly frequency — mp_to_quarterly
text: shock mp_shock object mp_shock() monthly frequency. method Character(1). One "sum" (default, usual choice additive shocks), "mean" (arithmetic mean months quarter), "end" (last month's value, useful stock-like measures shadow rates).

title: Aggregate a monthly shock series to quarterly frequency — mp_to_quarterly
text: data frame one row per quarter, containing date column set first day quarter, aggregated shock column, series identifier. Class c("mp_shock", "data.frame").

title: Aggregate a monthly shock series to quarterly frequency — mp_to_quarterly
text: Method selection. identified shocks (nakamura_steinsson, bauer_swanson, jarocinski_karadi_mp, miranda_agrippino_ricco), "sum" standard choice underlying objects additive surprises FOMC events. shadow rate (wu_xia), "end" returns end--quarter stance matches convention used zero-lower-bound regressions. "mean" appropriate dependent variable quarterly-average interest rate. NA handling. Missing monthly values dropped within quarter aggregation. whole quarter missing, result zero "sum" / "mean" NA "end".

what: mpshock: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics — mpshock-package
title: mpshock: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics — mpshock-package
text: Provides curated collection monetary policy shock stance series empirical macroeconomics literature, bundled tidy data frames provenance metadata. data bundled; runtime network access required.

title: mpshock: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics — mpshock-package
text: mp_shock() loads named series. mp_list() returns metadata table available series. mp_source() returns citation source URL series. mp_align() aligns series target data frame date. mp_to_quarterly() aggregates monthly series quarterly. mp_cumulate() computes cumulative rolling-window shock sums.

title: mpshock: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics — mpshock-package
text: United States: nakamura_steinsson: policy news shock, 2000-02 2014-03. bauer_swanson: orthogonalised MP surprise, 1988-02 2023-12. gss_target: GSS target factor (Swanson extended), 1991-07 2015-10. gss_path: GSS path factor (Swanson extended), 1991-07 2015-10. jarocinski_karadi_mp: pure MP shock, 1990-02 2024-01. jarocinski_karadi_cbi: CB information shock, 1990-02 2024-01. miranda_agrippino_ricco: informationally-robust MP shock, 1991-01 2019-06. wu_xia: shadow federal funds rate, 1960-01 2022-02. United Kingdom: ukmpd: three-factor UK event-study database (Target / Path / QE), 1997-06 latest BoE vintage. cesa_bianchi_uk: UK high-frequency surprise, 1997-06 2015-01. cloyne_hurtgen_uk: UK narrative shock, 1997-06 2009-02. Australia: hambur_haque_au: three-component RBA HFI shock (action / path / term premium), 2001-04 2019-12. beckers_au: RBA narrative shock (Bishop-Tulip + credit spreads), quarterly 1994-Q1 2018-Q4.

title: mpshock: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics — mpshock-package
text: general background shock identification, see Ramey (2016), "Macroeconomic Shocks Propagation," Handbook Macroeconomics 2: 71-162. recent cross-series comparison identification strategies bundled , see Aeberhardt, Bruno, Fidora (2024), "Monetary Policy Shocks: Data Methods?" FEDS Working Paper 2024-011.

title: mpshock: Monetary Policy Shock Series for Empirical Macroeconomics — mpshock-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com [copyright holder]

title: Nakamura-Steinsson policy news shock — nakamura_steinsson
text: monthly policy news shock series Nakamura Steinsson (2018). monthly observation sum high-frequency FOMC-event surprises occurring within month. surprise FOMC announcement first principal component changes five interest-rate futures (Fed Funds Eurodollar) 30-minute window bracketing announcement. Months scheduled FOMC meeting coded zero.

title: Nakamura-Steinsson policy news shock — nakamura_steinsson
Nakamura-Steinsson policy news shock — nakamura_steinsson
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. Policy news shock, scaled one-year Treasury-yield equivalents (percentage points). series character. Series identifier "nakamura_steinsson".

title: Nakamura-Steinsson policy news shock — nakamura_steinsson
text: Nakamura, E., & Steinsson, J. (2018). "High-Frequency Identification Monetary Non-Neutrality: Information Effect." Quarterly Journal Economics 133(3): 1283-1330. doi:10.1093/qje/qjy004 . Replication archive Harvard Dataverse: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/HZOXKN (CC0 1.0 Universal public domain dedication).

title: Nakamura-Steinsson policy news shock — nakamura_steinsson
text: Scaling. raw first principal component rescaled unit change equals contemporaneous change one-year nominal Treasury yield (NS Section II.B). Magnitudes therefore directly comparable Kuttner (2001) basis-point fed-funds surprises raw FF1 / FF4 surprises without rescaling. Interpretation caveat. NS frame policy-news shock evidence "Fed information effect": hawkish surprises raise private-sector growth forecasts. Bauer Swanson (2023, AER 113(3)) argue pattern better explained Fed professional forecasters reacting pre-meeting public data ("Fed response news"). Users estimating causal macro effects policy consider bauer_swanson (MPS_ORTH) miranda_agrippino_ricco alternatives address bias. Unscheduled meetings. Inter-meeting cuts (notably 22 January 2008 8 October 2008) included series drive large share sample variance.

what: UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (Braun-Miranda-Agrippino-Saha) — ukmpd
title: UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (Braun-Miranda-Agrippino-Saha) — ukmpd
text: UK equivalent Gurkaynak-Sack-Swanson three-factor decomposition: principal-component rotation high-frequency surprises OIS rates, gilt yields, short-sterling futures, FTSE 100 around Bank England MPC announcements Monetary Policy Report press conferences. Bundled monthly frequency; months MPC announcement coded zero.

title: UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (Braun-Miranda-Agrippino-Saha) — ukmpd
UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (Braun-Miranda-Agrippino-Saha) — ukmpd
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. Target factor (current Bank Rate surprise), percentage points. path numeric. Path factor (forward-guidance surprise), percentage points. qe numeric. QE factor (long-end asset-purchase surprise), percentage points. series character. Series identifier "ukmpd".

title: UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (Braun-Miranda-Agrippino-Saha) — ukmpd
text: Braun, R., Miranda-Agrippino, S., & Saha, T. (2025). "Measuring Monetary Policy UK: UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database." Journal Monetary Economics 149. doi:10.1016/j.jmoneco.2024.103645 . Data: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/working-paper/2023/measuring-monetary-policy---uk-ukmpd.

title: UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (Braun-Miranda-Agrippino-Saha) — ukmpd
text: Three factors. UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database (UKMPD) rotates three principal components Target (current Bank Rate surprise), Path (forward-guidance surprise), QE (long-end asset-purchase surprise). package bundles shock = Target, path qe additional columns users running multi-factor local projections. Maintenance. UKMPD flagship UK shock database: live- maintained authors published Bank England Staff Working Paper series. bundled version snapshot package build; check source URL latest vintage need observations bundled end date. Relation older UK series. UKMPD effectively supersedes Gerko-Rey (2017) UK surprises pre-MPR vintage cesa_bianchi_uk. Cesa-Bianchi-Thwaites-Vicondoa Cloyne-Hurtgen remain useful pre-1997 coverage narrative comparison.

what: Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate — wu_xia
title: Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate — wu_xia
text: monthly Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate Wu Xia (2016), maintained published Federal Reserve Bank Atlanta. shadow rate authors' estimate federal funds rate zero-lower-bound episodes (2008-12 2015-12 2020-03 2022-02) policy rates allowed go negative. companion effective federal funds rate (effr) included reference.

title: Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate — wu_xia
Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate — wu_xia
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day observation month. shock numeric. First difference shadow rate (percentage points per annum). shadow_rate numeric. Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate last business day month (percentage points per annum). effr numeric. Effective federal funds rate last business day month (percentage points per annum). series character. Series identifier "wu_xia".

title: Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate — wu_xia
text: Wu, J. C., & Xia, F. D. (2016). "Measuring Macroeconomic Impact Monetary Policy Zero Lower Bound." Journal Money, Credit Banking 48(2-3): 253-291. doi:10.1111/jmcb.12300 . Data: https://www.atlantafed.org/cqer/research/wu-xia-shadow-federal-funds-rate. US Federal Reserve research output; subject copyright 17 U.S.C. s. 105.

title: Wu-Xia shadow federal funds rate — wu_xia
text: Stance vs shock. shadow rate stance measure, policy shock. shock column first difference shadow_rate provided pipeline compatibility series package. conflates genuine policy news Kalman- filter revisions latent state. Users estimating shock IRFs prefer event-study series (nakamura_steinsson, bauer_swanson, miranda_agrippino_ricco) reserve wu_xia characterising zero-lower-bound policy stance. Model sensitivity. Krippner (2020, Journal Money, Credit Banking 52(4)) documents shadow-rate estimates sensitive choice effective lower bound, number factors (two versus three), set yield maturities used estimation. Wu-Xia's three-factor shadow-rate term-structure model (SRTSM) Krippner's two-factor SSR can differ 50 150 basis points 2014 2021 troughs. Results rely Wu-Xia alone replicated least one alternative shadow-rate series. Vintage. bundled series Atlanta Fed monthly update current package build. Historical values filtered estimates can change new data arrives; users needing fixed vintage download archived Atlanta Fed file directly.

title: mpshock 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 Initial release. Thirteen monetary policy shock stance series bundled across three countries.

title: mpshock 0.1.0
text: nakamura_steinsson: policy news shock Nakamura Steinsson (2018), monthly 2000-02 2014-03. Harvard Dataverse CC0 1.0. bauer_swanson: orthogonalised monetary policy surprise (MPS_ORTH) Bauer Swanson (2023), monthly 1988-02 2023-12. SF Fed research data. gss_target: Federal Funds Rate factor Swanson (2021) extension Gurkaynak, Sack, Swanson (2005), monthly 1991-07 2015-10. gss_path: Forward Guidance factor Swanson (2021) extension, monthly 1991-07 2015-10. jarocinski_karadi_mp: pure monetary policy shock Jarocinski Karadi (2020), monthly 1990-02 2024-01. jarocinski_karadi_cbi: central bank information shock Jarocinski Karadi (2020), monthly 1990-02 2024-01. miranda_agrippino_ricco: informationally-robust monetary policy shock Miranda-Agrippino Ricco (2021), monthly 1991-01 2019-06. wu_xia: shadow federal funds rate Wu Xia (2016), monthly 1960-01 2022-02. US Federal Reserve research output, public domain.

title: mpshock 0.1.0
text: ukmpd: UK Monetary Policy Event-Study Database Braun, Miranda-Agrippino, Saha (2025), three-factor decomposition (Target, Path, QE), monthly 1997-06 onwards, live-maintained Bank England. cesa_bianchi_uk: UK high-frequency surprise Cesa-Bianchi, Thwaites, Vicondoa (2020), monthly 1997-06 2015-01. cloyne_hurtgen_uk: UK narrative monetary policy shock Cloyne Hurtgen (2016), bundled via Cesa-Bianchi-Thwaites-Vicondoa re-compilation, monthly 1997-06 2009-02.

title: mpshock 0.1.0
text: hambur_haque_au: three-component Australian high-frequency surprise (action, path, term premium) Hambur Haque (2023, RDP 2023-04), monthly 2001-04 2019-12. RBA research output, CC 4.0. beckers_au: Australian narrative monetary policy shock (Bishop- Tulip plus credit spreads) Beckers (2020, RDP 2020-01), quarterly 1994-Q1 2018-Q4. RBA research output, CC 4.0.

title: mpshock 0.1.0
text: mp_shock() loads named series tidy data frame class mp_shock. mp_list() returns metadata table available series. mp_source() returns citation source URL series. mp_align() aligns series target data frame date. mp_to_quarterly() aggregates monthly series quarterly frequency. mp_cumulate() computes cumulative rolling-window shock sums. S3 print() format() methods mp_shock objects.
